patches_reviewed.subject: Re: [PATCH 0/9] Deliver TLS session tags to upper-layer consumers (NFSD)
patches_reviewed.patch_summary: This patch series aims to deliver TLS session tags to upper-layer consumers, specifically NFSD (Network File System Daemon), but it appears to have issues with applying cleanly to any known tree.

patches_acked.subject: Re: [PATCH 1/9] handshake: Require admin permission for DONE command

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH 0/3] fuse: io-uring request completion and ordering fixes

version_history.subject: [PATCH 0/3] fuse: io-uring request completion and ordering fixes

series_items.subject: [PATCH 1/3] fuse: fix EFAULT clobber in fuse_uring_commit
series_items.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the fuse_uring_commit function where an EFAULT error code is clobbered by a positive residual value from copy_from_user(). The fix involves assigning err = -EFAULT before jumping to out, ensuring that fuse_uring_req_end() receives a negative errno and sets req->out.h.error to -EFAULT.

review_comments.author: Bernd Schubert
review_comments.summary: Identified the same issue and suggested setting up a static check for copy_from_user() to avoid this problem in the future.
review_comments.raw_body:
On 6/5/26 21:27, Joanne Koong wrote:
> From: Chris Mason <clm@meta.com>
> 
> copy_from_user() returns the number of bytes not copied as an unsigned
> residual on failure (1..sizeof(struct fuse_out_header)). fuse_uring_commit
> stores that residual in ssize_t err, sets req->out.h.error to -EFAULT,
> then jumps to out: with err still holding the positive residual.
> 
>     err = copy_from_user(&req->out.h, &ent->headers->in_out,
>                          sizeof(req->out.h));
>     if (err) {
>         req->out.h.error = -EFAULT;
>         goto out;          /* err is the positive residual */
>     }
>     ...
>     out:
>         fuse_uring_req_end(ent, req, err);
> 
> fuse_uring_req_end() then runs
> 
>     if (error)
>         req->out.h.error = error;
> 
> which overwrites the just-assigned -EFAULT with the positive residual.
> FUSE callers such as fuse_simple_request() test err < 0 to detect
> failure, so the positive value is interpreted as success and the
> caller proceeds with an uninitialised or partial req->out.args.
> 
> Fix by assigning err = -EFAULT in the failure branch before jumping
> to out, so fuse_uring_req_end() receives a negative errno and sets
> req->out.h.error to -EFAULT.
> 
> Fixes: c090c8abae4b ("fuse: Add io-uring sqe commit and fetch support")
> Cc: stable@vger.kernel.org
> Reviewed-by: Joanne Koong <joannelkoong@gmail.com>
> Assisted-by: kres (claude-opus-4-7)
> Signed-off-by: Chris Mason <clm@meta.com>
> ---
>  fs/fuse/dev_uring.c | 9 +++------
>  1 file changed, 3 insertions(+), 6 deletions(-)
> 
> diff --git a/fs/fuse/dev_uring.c b/fs/fuse/dev_uring.c
> index e467b23e6895..e33847436693 100644
> --- a/fs/fuse/dev_uring.c
> +++ b/fs/fuse/dev_uring.c
> @@ -811,14 +811,11 @@ static void fuse_uring_commit(struct fuse_ring_ent *ent, struct fuse_req *req,
>  			      unsigned int issue_flags)
>  {
>  	struct fuse_ring *ring = ent->queue->ring;
> -	ssize_t err = 0;
> +	ssize_t err = -EFAULT;
>  
> -	err = copy_from_user(&req->out.h, &ent->headers->in_out,
> -			     sizeof(req->out.h));
> -	if (err) {
> -		req->out.h.error = -EFAULT;
> +	if (copy_from_user(&req->out.h, &ent->headers->in_out,
> +			   sizeof(req->out.h)))
>  		goto out;
> -	}
>  
>  	err = fuse_uring_out_header_has_err(&req->out.h, req);
>  	if (err) {

Oh right, I run every time into the same trap, need to set up some kind
of static check for {copy_from,to}_user.

Reviewed-by: Bernd Schubert <bernd@bsbernd.com>

series_items.subject: [PATCH 2/3] fuse: fix data races on ring->ready
series_items.patch_summary: This patch series addresses data races in the fuse kernel module by introducing synchronization primitives to ensure that operations on ring->ready are properly ordered. The first patch fixes a data race on ring->ready, while the second and third patches address related issues.

review_comments.author: Bernd Schubert
review_comments.summary: Provided a Reviewed-by tag without any specific comments or feedback.
review_comments.raw_body:
On 6/5/26 21:27, Joanne Koong wrote:
> From: Chris Mason <clm@meta.com>
> 
> On weakly-ordered architectures, the store to fiq->ops can be
> reordered past the store to ring->ready, allowing a CPU that sees
> ring->ready == true via fuse_uring_ready() to dispatch requests
> through a stale fiq->ops pointer. Upgrade the store to
> smp_store_release() and the load in fuse_uring_ready() to
> smp_load_acquire() so that the preceding WRITE_ONCE(fiq->ops, ...)
> is visible to any CPU that observes ring->ready == true.
> 
> Additionally, fuse_uring_do_register() publishes ring->ready with
> WRITE_ONCE() but the fast-path check reads it with a plain load.
> This is a marked-vs-unmarked access that KCSAN will flag. Wrap it in
> READ_ONCE() to mark it without adding unnecessary ordering.
> 
> Also wrap the fc->ring load in fuse_uring_ready() in READ_ONCE() to
> prevent the compiler from reloading it between the NULL check and the
> dereference.
> 
> Fixes: c2c9af9a0b13 ("fuse: Allow to queue fg requests through io-uring")
> Cc: stable@vger.kernel.org
> Reviewed-by: Joanne Koong <joannelkoong@gmail.com>
> Assisted-by: kres (claude-opus-4-7)
> Signed-off-by: Chris Mason <clm@meta.com>
> ---
>  fs/fuse/dev_uring.c   | 4 ++--
>  fs/fuse/dev_uring_i.h | 4 +++-
>  2 files changed, 5 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)
> 
> diff --git a/fs/fuse/dev_uring.c b/fs/fuse/dev_uring.c
> index e33847436693..7cd50990b097 100644
> --- a/fs/fuse/dev_uring.c
> +++ b/fs/fuse/dev_uring.c
> @@ -986,12 +986,12 @@ static void fuse_uring_do_register(struct fuse_ring_ent *ent,
>  	fuse_uring_ent_avail(ent, queue);
>  	spin_unlock(&queue->lock);
>  
> -	if (!ring->ready) {
> +	if (!READ_ONCE(ring->ready)) {
>  		bool ready = is_ring_ready(ring, queue->qid);
>  
>  		if (ready) {
>  			WRITE_ONCE(fiq->ops, &fuse_io_uring_ops);
> -			WRITE_ONCE(ring->ready, true);
> +			smp_store_release(&ring->ready, true);
>  			wake_up_all(&fch->blocked_waitq);
>  		}
>  	}
> diff --git a/fs/fuse/dev_uring_i.h b/fs/fuse/dev_uring_i.h
> index 368f4d0790eb..6af604e17b2d 100644
> --- a/fs/fuse/dev_uring_i.h
> +++ b/fs/fuse/dev_uring_i.h
> @@ -167,7 +167,9 @@ static inline void fuse_uring_wait_stopped_queues(struct fuse_chan *fch)
>  
>  static inline bool fuse_uring_ready(struct fuse_chan *fch)
>  {
> -	return fch->ring && fch->ring->ready;
> +	struct fuse_ring *ring = READ_ONCE(fch->ring);
> +
> +	return ring && smp_load_acquire(&ring->ready);
>  }
>  
>  #else /* CONFIG_FUSE_IO_URING */


Reviewed-by: Bernd Schubert <bernd@bsbernd.com>

series_items.subject: [PATCH 3/3] fuse: end fuse_req on io-uring cancel task work
series_items.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the io-uring implementation for FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) where a request is not properly ended when the task work is cancelled, leading to incorrect behavior and potential stalls. The fix involves calling fuse_uring_req_end() before falling through to fuse_uring_send() in the cancel branch of fuse_uring_send_in_task(). This ensures that the request is correctly ended and its reference is discharged, preventing issues such as repeated cancels inflating a counter and stalling background operations.

review_comments.author: Bernd Schubert
review_comments.summary: Reviewer Bernd Schubert noted a potential race condition between the proposed change and fuse_uring_stop_queues(), suggesting two possible solutions: either duplicating logic from fuse_uring_cancel() or refactoring through fuse_uring_entry_teardown().
review_comments.raw_body:
I think that can race with fuse_uring_stop_queues(), which leaves us two
choices


a) Same logic as fuse_uring_cancel() 
https://lore.kernel.org/r/20260515045541.1171335-4-joannelkoong@gmail.com

(also introduces slight code dup)

b) avoid the code dup and send it through fuse_uring_entry_teardown()
with a bit refactoring. I thought I had send an updated patch version
for that, but don't find it myself anymore. Explains why I never got
reply. The old patch from October had bug, but I'm rather that I
posted an mail with updated inline patch, not as separate series, 
though.


Thanks,
Bernd

review_comments.author: Joanne Koong (author)
review_comments.summary: Author Joanne Koong addressed the concern that her patch may introduce a race condition with fuse_uring_stop_queues(), but also acknowledged a potential issue with request expiration checking logic, agreeing to restructure in v2.
review_comments.raw_body:
Hmm, I don't think this races with fuse_uring_stop_queues() as
ent->state here is still FRRS_FUSE_REQ and fuse_uring_send_in_task()
can only be called for a registered fuse ent, which means the ent has
already grabbed the queue refcount which will trigger the async
teardown worker to run in the background during abort until the ent is
reclaimed. I think this adds a race though with the request expiration
checking logic which (a) fixed, so I think you're right that we'll
probably need the same cleanup here. I'll look at this early next week
and send a v2.

Thanks for taking a look at this series.

Thanks,
Joanne

review_comments.author: Bernd Schubert
review_comments.summary:
The reviewer pointed out that there is a use-after-free issue because the entry is set to FRRS_USERSPACE and then cleaned up during connection abort, which can lead to it being released again through fuse_uring_queues or fuse_uring_async_stop_queues.

The reviewer noted that the patch does not trigger fuse_dev_release() when a mean exit occurs, and questioned the necessity of the entire patch series.
review_comments.raw_body:
Right, actually no race at all, just a plain use-after-free, because the
entry is set to FRRS_USERSPACE and then cleaned up during connection
abort and then released again through fuse_uring_queues or
fuse_uring_async_stop_queues.

I actually do not get this part of the commit message

---

A mean exit does not trigger fuse_dev_release() with a fuse_abort_conn()
from that function?


I do not think we need 3/3 at all.


Thanks,
Bernd

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch series fixes three bugs in the io-uring implementation for FUSE file systems. The issues include a potential EFAULT error, data races on the ready ring, and incorrect handling of cancel tasks. The patches were identified by Chris Mason's AI program and are marked for stable inclusion.

review_comments.author: Bernd Schubert
review_comments.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch does not address a common issue of incorrect user-space pointer handling, specifically copy_from_user and copy_to_user functions, and suggested setting up static checks for these functions.
review_comments.raw_body:
Oh right, I run every time into the same trap, need to set up some kind
of static check for {copy_from,to}_user.

Reviewed-by: Bernd Schubert <bernd@bsbernd.com>

review_comments.summary: Author acknowledged that there is a potential race condition in the request expiration checking logic, agreed to restructure the code in v2 and planned to address it early next week.

ent->state here is still FRRS_FUSE_REQ and fuse_uring_send_in_task()
can only be called for a registered fuse ent, which means the ent has
already grabbed the queue refcount which will trigger the async

Thanks for taking a look at this series.

Thanks,
Joanne
